channel.title: Git on cekrem.github.io
channel.description: Recent content in Git on cekrem.github.io

item.title: git history: the best thing in Git 2.54
item.description:
Git 2.54 dropped yesterday. 137 contributors, 66 of them first-timers. But the thing I keep thinking about is a small experimental command called git history.

It does two things. git history reword <commit> lets you fix a commit message and rewrites everything downstream. git history split <commit> lets you chop a commit into pieces with the same hunk-picker from git add -p. Neither operation touches your working tree or index.

item.title: pretty-git-report – a CLI to give you daily commit metrics
item.description:
Have you ever… Link to heading

  • Wanted to track your daily additions and deletions without leaving your beloved terminal?
  • Busted your back piping git log {insert-hundred-flags} through endless hoops trying to create a somewhat human readable and useful output?
  • Wanted to use a functional node.js cli with an immoral amount of Rambda usage? (Sorry about that, btw, I just wanted to see how far I could go. Quite far, it turns out!)
  • Wished you would live to see a tool (any tool at all) that treverses a directory tree - :O – concurrently?

Then look no further. Lo and behold:
